title: Chickenpox treatment for kids: 10 herbal teas to help

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils, also known as tea tree oil, has been traditionally used to help alleviate the symptoms of chickenpox. The oil contains powerful properties like cineole, terpinen-4-ol, and limonene, which have antimicrobial, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory effects. These constituents help to reduce the severity of chickenpox by fighting off the underlying viral infection and soothing the skin.  To use Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils for chickenpox, dilute 2-3 drops of the oil in 2 tablespoons of carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. Gently apply the mixture to the affected skin, avoiding sensitive areas. You can also add a few drops of the oil to a warm bath or compress to help relax the body and reduce fever. Applying the oil to the affected skin can help to reduce the itching and inflammation associated with chickenpox, allowing the blisters to heal faster. The antimicrobial properties of the oil may also help to prevent secondary infections, which can be common in people with chickenpox. However, it's essential to note that Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils should not replace medical treatment, but rather be used in conjunction with standard care to help alleviate symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal essential oils for chickenpox

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis essential oils have been traditionally used to help soothe and calm the symptoms of chickenpox. The properties of calendula, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, make it effective in reducing redness, swelling, and the risk of infection associated with chickenpox. The essential oil contains constituents such as triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and carotenoids, which contribute to its healing properties.  To use calendula essential oils for chickenpox, you can add a few drops to a warm bath or apply it topically to the affected areas using a carrier oil. Mix 5-7 drops of calendula essential oil with 1 tablespoon of carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, and gently apply it to the itchy skin. You can also add calendula oil to a warm compress and apply it to the affected areas to help reduce itching and promote healing.  When using calendula essential oils for chickenpox, you may notice a reduction in itching and inflammation within a few days. The oil may also help to promote the healing of blisters and prevent scarring. However, it's essential to note that calendula essential oils should not be used as a replacement for medical treatment, but rather as a complementary therapy to help manage symptoms. Always consult with a healthcare professional before using essential oils, especially if you have a weakened immune system or are pregnant or breastfeeding.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal essential oils for chickenpox

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils contain properties that can help soothe and calm the skin, making it a potential natural remedy for chickenpox. The main constituents of this oil include linalool and linalyl acetate, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. These properties can help reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with chickenpox, and also prevent infection by killing bacteria on the affected skin. To use Lavandula angustifolia essential oils for chickenpox, mix a few drops with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil and gently apply it to the affected areas. You can also add a few drops to a warm bath to help relax and calm your child. However, make sure to consult a healthcare professional before using essential oils, especially if your child has a weakened immune system or is taking any medications. When applied correctly, Lavandula angustifolia essential oils can help reduce the severity and duration of chickenpox symptoms. It may also help promote healing and prevent scarring. Some parents have reported a reduction in the number of blisters and a decrease in the intensity of itching and pain. However, it's essential to note that essential oils are not a substitute for medical treatment, and your child should still be under the care of a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oils for chickenpox

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis essential oils have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help soothe and calm the skin during a chickenpox outbreak. It contains constituents like aloe-emodin, aloin, and vitamins A, C, and E, which have been shown to reduce redness, itching, and swelling. When using Aloe barbadensis essential oils for chickenpox, apply a few drops to the affected skin area and gently massage it in. You can also mix it with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil to dilute its potency. It's essential to note that Aloe barbadensis essential oils should be used in moderation and under adult supervision for children.  As the oil works to reduce inflammation, you may start to see a decrease in the severity of symptoms within a few hours. The itching and redness may subside, and the skin may appear less inflamed. However, it's crucial to remember that chickenpox is a viral infection and can only be treated with rest, hydration, and over-the-counter medications to help manage symptoms. Aloe barbadensis essential oils can be used as a complementary remedy to provide relief from discomfort, but it's not a replacement for medical treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal essential oils for chickenpox

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils contain compounds like eucalyptol, limonene, and alpha-pinene, which have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. These properties may help soothe itchy skin, reduce fever, and combat the underlying viral infection that causes chickenpox. When used topically, Eucalyptus globulus essential oils may help ease the discomfort associated with chickenpox by reducing redness, swelling, and the characteristic rash. To use Eucalyptus globulus essential oils for chickenpox, dilute a few drops of the oil with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil and apply it to the affected areas with a cotton ball. You can also add a few drops to your bath water for a relaxing and therapeutic soak.  However, it's essential to note that Eucalyptus globulus essential oils should not be ingested and should be used with caution, as they can cause skin irritation in some individuals. Also, it's crucial to consult with a healthcare professional before using essential oils, especially if you're pregnant, breastfeeding, or taking medications.  When using Eucalyptus globulus essential oils for chickenpox, you may experience a reduction in the severity and duration of the rash, a decrease in fever, and an improvement in overall comfort. The itchy skin may become less irritated, and the characteristic blisters may heal faster. However, it's crucial to continue following your healthcare provider's advice and taking any prescribed medications to ensure the best possible outcome.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal essential oils for chickenpox

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea essential oils have been traditionally used to help alleviate the symptoms of chickenpox. This is because it contains properties such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The constituents, including cis-3-hexenal and cis-3-hexenyl acetate, can help to reduce the severity and duration of the rash.  To use Echinacea purpurea essential oils for chickenpox, mix a few drops with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil and apply it topically to the affected areas. You can also add a few drops to a warm bath for a relaxing soak. Some people also use it in aromatherapy by inhaling the oil directly from the bottle or adding it to a diffuser. When using Echinacea purpurea essential oils for chickenpox, you may notice a reduction in the number and severity of the blisters, as well as a decrease in the fever and general feeling of being unwell. The oil may also help to promote healing and reduce the risk of further complications. However, it's essential to note that Echinacea purpurea essential oils should not replace medical treatment for chickenpox, but rather be used in conjunction with conventional treatment. It's also crucial to consult with a healthcare professional before using any essential oils, especially if you have a weakened immune system or are pregnant or breastfeeding.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal essential oils for chickenpox

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera essential oils have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that can help soothe and calm chickenpox symptoms. The oil contains constituents like aloe-emodin, aloin, and vitamins A, C, and E, which work together to reduce redness, itching, and swelling. When applied topically, aloe vera essential oil can help to: Apply a few drops of aloe vera essential oil to the affected areas, mixed with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, and gently massage it in. You can also add a few drops to your bath water for a relaxing soak. Be sure to dilute the essential oil with a carrier oil, as it can be too potent for direct application. By using aloe vera essential oil, you may experience relief from intense itching and discomfort, reduced inflammation, and improved wound healing. The oil's antimicrobial properties can also help prevent infection and promote a faster recovery. However, it's essential to note that aloe vera essential oil is not a substitute for medical treatment, and you should consult a doctor before using it as a treatment for chickenpox.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe vera herbal essential oils for chickenpox

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum essential oils, also known as St. John's Wort, have been traditionally used to help with chickenpox. This is because the oil contains properties like anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antiseptic compounds, which can help soothe and heal the skin. The constituents of the oil, including flavonoids and terpenoids, have been shown to reduce inflammation and fight off viral infections.  When using Hypericum perforatum essential oils for chickenpox, it's essential to dilute the oil with a carrier oil and apply it topically to the affected areas. You can add a few drops of the oil to a warm bath or mix it with a moisturizer for direct application. Gently massage the oil into the skin, making sure to avoid broken skin and sensitive areas.  As the oil is absorbed into the skin, you may notice a reduction in the severity of symptoms, such as itching and inflammation. The antiviral properties of the oil can help combat the virus, reducing the duration and frequency of the outbreaks. The oil may also promote wound healing, reducing the risk of infection and scarring. However, it's crucial to note that while Hypericum perforatum essential oils can provide relief, they may not cure the condition entirely.
remedies.image_alt: Hypericum perforatum herbal essential oils for chickenpox

intro_desc: Chickenpox is a common illness in children that causes a painful and itchy rash. It's usually caused by the varicella-zoster virus, which can spread easily from person to person through the air or through direct contact with someone who's infected. When a child gets chickenpox, it can be really uncomfortable for them - they may feel tired, have a fever, and feel really itchy all over. This can make it hard for them to sleep, eat, and even play with their friends. The rash itself can be quite painful, especially when it starts to blister and crust over. This can be really frustrating for kids, and it can also be hard for parents to watch their child feel so unwell.  To make matters worse, chickenpox can be a real disruption to a child's life. They may have to stay home from school or daycare, which can be boring and isolating. They may also have to miss out on fun activities with friends and family, which can be really disappointing. And of course, there's the physical discomfort of the rash itself, which can be really painful and itchy. All of this can be really tough on a child's emotional and mental health.  But there is some good news - there are some natural ways to help ease the symptoms of chickenpox. Herbal essential oils can be a great help in this area. These oils are made from plants and can be used to promote relaxation, reduce itching and inflammation, and even help to speed up the healing process. By using these oils in a safe and gentle way, parents can help their child feel more comfortable and relaxed while they recover from chickenpox. This can make a big difference in a child's day-to-day life and help them feel more like themselves again.
